seo.title: Content Platforms & CMS implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Content Platforms & CMS projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist outlines the technical requirements for deploying a headless CMS to production, focusing on schema integrity, API performance, security, and editorial efficiency.

items.title: Define Field-Level Validations
items.description: Configure regex patterns, character limits, and range constraints for all text and numeric fields to prevent database corruption and UI breakage.

items.title: Establish Reference Depth Limits
items.description: Audit nested relationships to ensure circular references are blocked and depth limits are set to prevent payload bloat in API responses.

items.title: Implement Slug Uniqueness Constraints
items.description: Verify that URL slug fields are unique across the content type and include automated transformation logic to handle special characters.

items.title: Configure Localization Strategy
items.description: Define field-level vs. entry-level localization and verify that fallback locales are correctly configured for multi-regional deployments.

items.title: Required Field Enforcement
items.description: Flag all fields necessary for frontend rendering as 'Required' within the CMS UI to prevent null-pointer errors in the application layer.

items.title: Configure CDN and Cache Purging
items.description: Set up stale-while-revalidate (SWR) headers and verify that the CMS triggers webhook-based cache purges on content publication.

items.title: Implement GraphQL Query Complexity Analysis
items.description: Restrict query depth and complexity scores for public endpoints to prevent Denial of Service (DoS) via deep content nesting.

items.title: Batch API Requests
items.description: Verify that the frontend implementation uses batched requests or fragments to minimize the number of round-trips to the CMS API.

items.title: Set Rate Limits
items.description: Apply rate limiting on API keys used by client-side applications to prevent exhaustion of monthly CMS usage quotas.

items.title: Monitor Response Payloads
items.description: Audit JSON responses to ensure unused fields are excluded from the output via field selection or projections.

items.title: Rotate Production API Keys
items.description: Ensure production environments use distinct read-only keys and that management tokens are stored in secure environment variables.

items.title: Verify Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
items.description: Test that editor roles cannot access schema settings and that content-specific permissions restrict users to their assigned sections.

items.title: Configure CORS Policies
items.description: Restrict the CMS API access to specific production domains and authorized preview environments.

items.title: Validate Webhook Signatures
items.description: Implement signature verification in the application receiver to ensure incoming CMS webhooks are authentic and not spoofed.

items.title: Environment Isolation
items.description: Ensure development and staging data are stored in separate CMS spaces or environments to prevent accidental production data overwrites.

items.title: Deploy Live Preview Environment
items.description: Configure a dedicated preview URL that renders draft content using the CMS's preview API tokens for real-time editorial feedback.

items.title: Standardize Content States
items.description: Define clear workflow stages (e.g., Draft, In Review, Ready) and ensure the frontend logic only fetches 'Published' items by default.

items.title: Implement AI Guardrails
items.description: If using AI generation, create system prompts that enforce brand voice and length constraints within the CMS UI extension.

items.title: Configure SEO Metadata Fields
items.description: Mandate meta-titles, descriptions, and Open Graph image fields across all page-level content types.

items.title: Enable Content Versioning
items.description: Verify that history tracking is active to allow editors to revert to previous versions of content entries.

items.title: Enforce Alt-Text Requirements
items.description: Set the alt-text field as a mandatory attribute for all image assets within the media library.

items.title: Configure Image Transformation Parameters
items.description: Standardize the use of URL-based transformations (e.g., auto-format, quality settings) to ensure optimal image delivery.

items.title: Sanitize SVG Uploads
items.description: Ensure that the CMS or the upload pipeline strips scripts and harmful attributes from SVG files to prevent XSS attacks.

items.title: Set Asset Upload Limits
items.description: Define maximum file size limits for assets to prevent editors from uploading unoptimized high-resolution media.

items.title: Implement Folder Structure
items.description: Organize the media library into logical directories (e.g., /blog, /marketing, /products) to facilitate asset discovery.

items.title: Automate Schema Backups
items.description: Schedule daily exports of the CMS schema and content entries to an external storage bucket (e.g., S3 or GCS).

items.title: Setup Migration Scripts
items.description: Use version-controlled migration scripts (e.g., Contentful Migrations or Sanity Schema) rather than manual UI changes for schema updates.

items.title: Check for Broken Links
items.description: Run a crawler against the preview environment to detect internal links pointing to 404s or unpublished content.

items.title: Configure Redirect Management
items.description: Ensure a mechanism exists (either in-CMS or via edge middleware) to handle 301 redirects for modified slugs.

items.title: Audit Third-Party Plugins
items.description: Review and update all CMS plugins or UI extensions to ensure compatibility with the current CMS core version.
